generatedSentence: Elara, a lovable master of peculiar math, found her daily work fulfilling. Her routine involved sketching with trusty pencils, her knapsack always near. She enjoyed walking through the city's galleries, admiring an antique knickknack. Her modest earnings allowed her to enjoy life. In the meantime, she was eager to explore her unique lineage. She was, after all, a true homeboy to the city, feeling deeply connected to its hidden history. Suddenly, her comms rattled – a clear sign of trouble. An imposter was making the city's power grid go haywire. It was an urgent situation; many underestimated the threat. A strange poison had been introduced into the central generator's core, rendering it a pitiful mess. Elara spotted a black mustang driving erratically, and behind it, a vintage hummer, both seemingly borrowed. Whoever this was, they aimed to interrupt the city’s peace, leaving a digital potato chip on every radio frequency. Elara knew she couldn't remain invisible any longer. In a blur of haste, Elara activated her getaway protocol, a system of inductor coils she had interfaced with. The true villain, a chilling watcher cloaked in the artificial glow of krypton, revealed himself. He declared himself the unrivaled ruler of this digital expanse. Meanwhile, the city's brotherhood of peacekeepers, led by the wise bishop, were being held at a remote barbecue plant – a strategic knockdown. Elara swore to be a witness to his downfall, standing benevolent against his tyranny, feeling like a digital groundhog emerging to face a storm. With the system fully upgrading and the digital hickory of the network cleansed, Elara felt ecstatic. The threat was now under control, and the city’s heart worked again. She understood that each corrupted molecule was part of the rarest digital plague. As dawn broke, she watched a herd of metallic gazelles sprint across the digital labyrinth she had secured. From her luggage, she pulled a lilac-colored pistachio and a small kanji charm, then began to recite a peaceful rasta chant, finally able to fully enjoy the tranquility she had restored.
panelSentences: Elara, a lovable master of peculiar math, found her daily work fulfilling. Her routine involved sketching with trusty pencils, her knapsack always near. She enjoyed walking through the city's galleries, admiring an antique knickknack. Her modest earnings allowed her to enjoy life. In the meantime, she was eager to explore her unique lineage. She was, after all, a true homeboy to the city, feeling deeply connected to its hidden history.

Suddenly, her comms rattled – a clear sign of trouble. An imposter was making the city's power grid go haywire. It was an urgent situation; many underestimated the threat. A strange poison had been introduced into the central generator's core, rendering it a pitiful mess. Elara spotted a black mustang driving erratically, and behind it, a vintage hummer, both seemingly borrowed. Whoever this was, they aimed to interrupt the city’s peace, leaving a digital potato chip on every radio frequency. Elara knew she couldn't remain invisible any longer.

In a blur of haste, Elara activated her getaway protocol, a system of inductor coils she had interfaced with. The true villain, a chilling watcher cloaked in the artificial glow of krypton, revealed himself. He declared himself the unrivaled ruler of this digital expanse. Meanwhile, the city's brotherhood of peacekeepers, led by the wise bishop, were being held at a remote barbecue plant – a strategic knockdown. Elara swore to be a witness to his downfall, standing benevolent against his tyranny, feeling like a digital groundhog emerging to face a storm.

With the system fully upgrading and the digital hickory of the network cleansed, Elara felt ecstatic. The threat was now under control, and the city’s heart worked again. She understood that each corrupted molecule was part of the rarest digital plague. As dawn broke, she watched a herd of metallic gazelles sprint across the digital labyrinth she had secured. From her luggage, she pulled a lilac-colored pistachio and a small kanji charm, then began to recite a peaceful rasta chant, finally able to fully enjoy the tranquility she had restored.
rawPanelSentences: Elara, a **lovable** **master** of peculiar **math**, found her daily **work** fulfilling. Her routine involved sketching with trusty **pencils**, her **knapsack** always near. She enjoyed **walking** through the city's **galleries**, admiring an antique **knickknack**. Her modest **earnings** allowed her to **enjoy** life. In the **meantime**, she was **eager** to explore her unique **lineage**. She was, after all, a true **homeboy** to the city, feeling deeply connected to its hidden history.

Suddenly, her comms **rattled** – a clear sign of trouble. An **imposter** was making the city's power grid go **haywire**. It was an **urgent** situation; many **underestimated** the threat. A strange **poison** had been introduced into the central **generator**'s core, rendering it a **pitiful** mess. Elara spotted a black **mustang** driving erratically, and behind it, a vintage **hummer**, both seemingly **borrowed**. Whoever this was, they aimed to **interrupt** the city’s peace, leaving a digital **potato** chip on every **radio** frequency. Elara knew she couldn't remain **invisible** any longer.

In a blur of **haste**, Elara activated her **getaway** protocol, a system of **inductor** coils she had **interfaced** with. The true villain, a chilling **watcher** cloaked in the artificial glow of **krypton**, revealed himself. He declared himself the **unrivaled** ruler of this digital **expanse**. Meanwhile, the city's **brotherhood** of peacekeepers, led by the wise **bishop**, were being held at a remote **barbecue** plant – a strategic **knockdown**. Elara swore to be a **witness** to his downfall, standing **benevolent** against his tyranny, feeling like a digital **groundhog** emerging to face a storm.

With the system fully **upgrading** and the digital **hickory** of the network cleansed, Elara felt **ecstatic**. The threat was now **under** control, and the city’s heart **worked** again. She understood that each corrupted **molecule** was part of the **rarest** digital plague. As dawn broke, she watched a herd of metallic **gazelles** sprint across the digital **labyrinth** she had secured. From her **luggage**, she pulled a **lilac**-colored **pistachio** and a small **kanji** charm, then began to **recite** a peaceful **rasta** chant, finally able to fully **enjoy** the tranquility she had restored.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, neo-noir sci-fi comic art style, thick white gutters, clean black borders, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. A cybernetically enhanced detective wearing a worn trench coat, illuminated goggles, and a wide-brimmed fedora. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elara sketching intricate designs in her dimly lit workshop, surrounded by blueprints and holographic projections, holding a pencil, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A vintage hummer and a black mustang speeding past a towering, glitching digital billboard displaying distorted radio static, low angle shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elara confronting a cloaked villain (the watcher) in a server room bathed in the artificial glow of krypton, with bound brotherhood members and a benevolent bishop in the background, dynamic action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elara relaxing peacefully, watching a herd of metallic gazelles sprint across a serene digital landscape at dawn, holding a lilac-colored pistachio and a small kanji charm from her luggage, wide shot.

definitions.definition: A paintbrush.
A writing utensil with a graphite (commonly referred to as lead) shaft, usually blended with clay, clad in wood, and sharpened to a taper.
An aggregate or collection of rays of light, especially when diverging from, or converging to, a point.
A family of geometric objects with a common property, such as the set of lines that pass through a given point in a projective plane.
A small medicated bougie.
To write (something) using a pencil.

definitions.definition: To mark with, or as if with, a pencil.
The tuber of a plant, Solanum tuberosum, eaten as a starchy vegetable, particularly in the Americas and Europe; this plant.
A conspicuous hole in a sock or stocking
A camera that takes poor-quality pictures.
A mentally handicapped person.
An underpowered computer or other device, especially when small in size.
Feeling pity; merciful.
So appalling or sad that one feels or should feel sorry for it; eliciting pity.

definitions.definition: Of an amount or number: very small.

definitions.definition: In a pitiful manner; pitifully; piteously; pathetically.
A deciduous tree (Pistacia vera) grown in parts of Asia for its drupaceous fruit.
The nutlike fruit of this tree.
(color) A pale green colour, like that of a pistachio seed.
A substance that is harmful or lethal to a living organism.

definitions.definition: Something that harms a person or thing.

definitions.definition: A drink; liquor.
definitions.example: — I'll have a glass of whisky.

definitions.definition: Any substance that inhibits catalytic activity.
To use poison to kill or paralyse (somebody).
definitions.example: The assassin poisoned the king.

definitions.definition: To pollute; to cause to become poisonous.
definitions.example: That factory is poisoning the river.

definitions.definition: To cause to become much worse.
definitions.example: He poisoned the mood in the room with his non-stop criticism.

definitions.definition: To cause (someone) to hate or to have unfair negative opinions.
definitions.example: She's poisoned him against all his old friends.

definitions.definition: To inhibit the catalytic activity of.
To place false information into (a cache) as part of an exploit.
(heading) Employment.
(heading) Effort.
Sustained effort to achieve a goal or result, especially overcoming obstacles.
definitions.example: We don't have much time. Let's get to work piling up those sandbags.

definitions.definition: (heading) Product; the result of effort.
The staging of events to appear as real.
Ore before it is dressed.
The equipment needed to inject a drug (syringes, needles, swabs etc.)
definitions.example: Tell me you're using clean works at least.

definitions.definition: To do a specific task by employing physical or mental powers.
definitions.example: He’s working in a bar.

definitions.definition: To effect by gradual degrees.
definitions.example: he worked his way through the crowd

definitions.definition: To embroider with thread.
To set into action.
definitions.example: He worked the levers.

definitions.definition: To cause to ferment.
To ferment.
To exhaust, by working.
definitions.example: The mine was worked until the last scrap of ore had been extracted.

definitions.definition: To shape, form, or improve a material.
definitions.example: He used pliers to work the wire into shape.

definitions.definition: To operate in a certain place, area, or speciality.
definitions.example: she works the night clubs

definitions.definition: To operate in or through; as, to work the phones.
To provoke or excite; to influence.
definitions.example: The rock musician worked the crowd of young girls into a frenzy.

definitions.definition: To use or manipulate to one’s advantage.
definitions.example: She knows how to work the system.

definitions.definition: To cause to happen or to occur as a consequence.
definitions.example: I cannot work a miracle.

definitions.definition: To cause to work.

definitions.definition: To function correctly; to act as intended; to achieve the goal designed for.
definitions.example: he pointed at the car and asked, "Does it work"?;  he looked at the bottle of pain pills, wondering if they would work;  my plan didn’t work

definitions.definition: To influence.

definitions.definition: To effect by gradual degrees; as, to work into the earth.
To move in an agitated manner.
definitions.example: A ship works in a heavy sea.

definitions.definition: To behave in a certain way when handled
definitions.example: this dough does not work easily;  the soft metal works well

definitions.definition: (with two objects) To cause (someone) to feel (something); to do unto somebody (something, whether good or bad).
To hurt; to ache.
Designed or executed in a particular manner or to a particular degree.
Wrought.
Prepared so as to demonstrate the steps required.
To move on the feet by alternately setting each foot (or pair or group of feet, in the case of animals with four or more feet) forward, with at least one foot on the ground at all times. Compare run.
definitions.example: To walk briskly for an hour every day is to keep fit.

definitions.definition: To "walk free", i.e. to win, or avoid, a criminal court case, particularly when actually guilty.
definitions.example: If you can’t present a better case, that robber is going to walk.

definitions.definition: Of an object, to go missing or be stolen.
definitions.example: If you leave your wallet lying around, it’s going to walk.

definitions.definition: (of a batsman) To walk off the field, as if given out, after the fielding side appeals and before the umpire has ruled; done as a matter of sportsmanship when the batsman believes he is out.
To travel (a distance) by walking.
definitions.example: I walk two miles to school every day.  The museum’s not far from here – you can walk it.

definitions.definition: To allow a batter to reach base by pitching four balls.
To move something by shifting between two positions, as if it were walking.

definitions.definition: To full; to beat cloth to give it the consistency of felt.
To traverse by walking (or analogous gradual movement).

definitions.definition: To operate the left and right throttles of (an aircraft) in alternation.
To leave, resign.

definitions.definition: To push (a vehicle) alongside oneself as one walks.
To behave; to pursue a course of life; to conduct oneself.

To be in motion; to act; to move.

definitions.definition: Someone who watches or observes.

A guard.

definitions.definition: The technology that allows for the transmission of sound or other signals by modulation of electromagnetic waves.

To adopt (an idea) as one's own.

definitions.definition: To adopt a word from another language.

(Upper Midwestern United States) To lend.
To temporarily obtain (something) for (someone).
To feign or counterfeit.
(obsolete except in ballads) To secure the release of (someone) from prison.
To receive (something) from somebody, with little possibility of returning it.
definitions.example: Can I borrow a sheet of paper?

definitions.definition: A fireplace or pit for grilling food, typically used outdoors and traditionally employing hot charcoal as the heating medium.
definitions.example: We cooked our food on the barbecue.

definitions.definition: A meal or event highlighted by food cooked in such an apparatus.
definitions.example: We're having a barbecue on Saturday, and you're invited.

definitions.definition: Meat, especially pork or beef, which has been cooked in such an apparatus (i.e. smoked over indirect heat from high-smoke fuels) and then chopped up or shredded.

A floor on which coffee beans are sun-dried.
A framework of sticks.
To cook food on a barbecue; to smoke it over indirect heat from high-smoke fuels.
To grill.

Any watchman, inspector, or overlooker.
A chief of the Festival of Fools or St. Nicholas Day.
The chess piece denoted ♗ or ♝ which moves along diagonal lines and developed from the shatranj alfil ("elephant") and was originally known as the aufil or archer in English.
Any of various African birds of the genus Euplectes; a kind of weaverbird closely related to the widowbirds.
A ladybug or ladybird, beetles of the family Coccinellidae.
A sweet drink made from wine, usually with oranges, lemons, and sugar; mulled and spiced port.
A bustle.
A children's smock or pinafore.
To act as a bishop, to perform the duties of a bishop, especially to confirm another's membership in the church.
To make a bishop.
To provide with bishops.
To permit food (especially milk) to burn while cooking (from bishops' role in the inquisition or as mentioned in the quotation below, of horses).
(by extension, of horses) To make a horse seem younger, particularly by manipulation of its teeth.
The state of being brothers or a brother.
An association for any purpose, such as a society of monks; a fraternity.
The whole body of persons engaged in the same business, especially those of the same profession
definitions.example: the legal brotherhood

definitions.definition: People, or (poetically) things, of the same kind.
Having a disposition to do good.
definitions.example: Chinese and Eastern mythologies describe dragons as benevolent.

definitions.definition: Possessing or manifesting love for mankind.
Altruistic, charitable, good, just and fair.
Beyond compare, far surpassing any other, unparalleled, without rival.
definitions.example: Helen's unrivaled beauty is said to have launched a thousand ships.

definitions.definition: Requiring immediate attention.
definitions.example: An urgent appeal was sent out for assistance.

definitions.definition: To perceive (someone or something) as having a lower value, quantity, worth, etc., than what he/she/it actually has.
To improve, usually applied to technology, generally by complete replacement of one or more components
definitions.example: When you upgrade your standard of living, you become addicted to that standard of living.

definitions.definition: To replace with something better.
definitions.example: I had to upgrade my anti-virus software to protect my computer from the newer threats.

definitions.definition: To improve the equipment or furnishings of or services rendered to
definitions.example: They upgraded him to the latest model.

definitions.definition: To improve in condition or status.
definitions.example: She upgraded to a more successful husband.

definitions.definition: To replace a program with a later version of itself, a version having a higher version number or marketed under a more recent product name.
The process by which something is upgraded.
Being lower; being beneath something.
Under anesthesia, especially general anesthesia; sedated.
definitions.example: Ensure the patient is sufficiently under.

definitions.definition: In a way lower or less than.
In a way inferior to.
In an unconscious state.
definitions.example: It took the hypnotist several minutes to make his subject go under.

definitions.definition: In or at a lower level than.
As a subject of; subordinate to.
definitions.example: He served in World War II under General Omar Bradley.

definitions.definition: Less than.
Below the surface of.
In the face of; in response to (some attacking force).
definitions.example: to collapse under stress; to give in under interrogation

definitions.definition: As, in the character of.
definitions.example: he writes books under the name John Smith

definitions.definition: Descent in a line from a common progenitor; progeny; descending line of offspring or ascending line of parentage.
A number of lines of text in a column.
Inspiring or deserving love or affection.
A maze-like structure built by Daedalus in Knossos, containing the Minotaur.
Part of the inner ear.
Anything complicated and confusing, like a maze.
Any of various satyrine butterflies of the genus Neope.
To enclose in a labyrinth, or as though in a labyrinth.
To arrange in the form of a labyrinth.
To twist and wind, following a labyrinthine path.
To render lost and confused, as if in a labyrinth.
A large shrub of the genus Syringa, especially Syringa vulgaris, bearing white, pale-pink or purple flowers.
A flower of the lilac shrub.
(color) A pale purple color, the color of some lilac flowers.
Having a pale purple colour.
The bags and other containers that hold a traveller's belongings.
The contents of such containers.
A specific bag or container holding a traveller's belongings.
